question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any urgent action being taken to resolve the issue of sewage flowing into Bangor marina.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water has advised that it has uncovered a severe blockage at Castle Park Wastewater Pumping Station which caused a sewage discharge to Bangor marina. The blockage is mainly made up of sanitary items, baby wipes and fats, oils and grease.  This pumping station also has quite a significant storm storage and it was a discharge from the storm side of the well that caused the discolouration in the marina.  However, NI Water has reduced the levels and the discharge has now stopped.  It is important to clarify that the storm discharge from the pumping station enters the culverted Clandeboye River and it is this which permanently discharges into the marina.  This culvert is owned by a third party.  However, on this occasion, NI Water will check to ensure that any sewage related debris from this spillage is removed.  While the extensive blockage has now been located, the work to remove it is complex and is weather dependent.   Once the blockage is cleared, NI Water will increase attendance at the site for a time, to help ensure the problem doesn’t reoccur in the near future. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of whether the PSNI will be given a pay award in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: I am actively considering the recommendations from the Police Remuneration Review Body.  All pay awards must be considered in the context of the NI Public Sector Pay Policy which stipulates that they must be affordable within each department’s 2024-25 budget settlement.  I remain committed to working with the Department of Finance and the PSNI to progress payment to officers as soon as possible.It should be noted that on 6 September my Department approved the pay remit that will allow the PSNI to implement incremental progression to police officers’ salaries for those entitled.  It is anticipated that Officers should see payments in September payroll.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he plans to provide additional funding to repair potholes in the Ards and North Down District Council area.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. In response, my Department has had to reduce its road maintenance activities to a limited service that prioritises delivery of essential services for maintaining public safety and protecting the transport network. Consequently, only the highest-priority defects across the network are currently being repaired.Recognising the vital need for investment in our road infrastructure, I allocated £89 million of capital funding to structural maintenance this financial year and I continue to bid for additional capital funding to invest in the road infrastructure. In addition, I have allocated £38m of resource funding to support delivery of a essential maintenance service which prioritises the repair of those defects that present the greatest road safety risk to our road users. My Department continues to repair the highest priority defects across the road network, including in the Ards and North Down District Council area. However, I am well aware of the frustrations and, at times, inconvenience that poor road conditions have caused. To address this, for a number of years now my Department has allocated budget to a Roads Recovery Fund, which is primarily aimed at improving the lower tier network. These improvements target many short lengths of mainly rural roads that are in particularly poor condition and aims to see a positive impact on road conditions. In 2024/25, I have allocated some £10m to the Roads Recovery Fund across use across all council areas.In addition to this, I have agreed to the introduction of a pilot scheme in the Ards & North Down Borough Council area that ensures a full programme of road inspections are completed and that any identified defects are repaired promptly. Through measures such as this, it is hoped to see an overall improvement in the condition of the road network despite the difficult financial situation.
